title: heterosperma pinnatum var. involucratum
intro: Heterosperma pinnatum var. involucratum is a plant species commonly known as Mexican daisy, belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its daisy-like flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Heterosperma pinnatum var. involucratum include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its antioxidant properties, which may help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used topically as a poultice or cream to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
